title: NOC 84100 – Underground mine service and support workers | GTR Immigration
description: Underground mine service and support workers under NOC 84100 are the operational backbone of Canada's subterranean resource sector. As a TEER 4 occupation, these professionals perform the essential technical and safety support tasks—such as installing ventilation, managing water systems, and reinforcing rock walls—that allow production miners to work safely. In 2026, with Canada’s "Critical Minerals Strategy" driving the construction of new copper and nickel mines, these support workers are in record-high demand. For immigration, this role is a primary candidate for the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP) and the new 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level4Major Group84 – Workers in natural resources, agriculture and related productionSub-Major Group841 – Workers in natural resources, agriculture and related productionMinor Group8410 – Workers in natural resources, agriculture and related productionNOC Code84100

duties: Attach and extend air and water pipes and ventilation ducts to support the mining face.
Install and maintain underground support structures, including rock bolts, wire mesh, and timbering to ensure wall stability.
Operate specialized machinery such as scissor lifts, mucking machines, or small utility vehicles to transport materials.
Assist blasters by carrying explosives to the working face and helping to clear debris after a blast.
Maintain and clear underground tracks, roadways, and drainage ditches.
Operate equipment to provide fire protection and emergency response support within the mine.
Perform routine maintenance on service equipment and report safety hazards to supervisors.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
On-the-Job Training: Formal training of several months is provided by the employer.

Certifications: * Completion of the Basic Common Core program (mandatory in Ontario).First Aid Certification is a standard requirement.WHMIS and specialized safety training for underground environments.
First Aid Certification is a standard requirement.
WHMIS and specialized safety training for underground environments.
Physical stamina and the ability to work in confined, remote environments on a shift-rotation basis (e.g., 2 weeks on / 2 weeks off) are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: Total compensation for "Fly-In-Fly-Out" (FIFO) workers often includes significant overtime, production bonuses, and travel allowances.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. With billions of dollars in new investment flowing into Canada's battery-metal mines (Lithium, Nickel, Cobalt), the demand for support infrastructure is higher than it has been in decades. Industry projections suggest that for every production miner hired, at least two support workers are needed to maintain the safe operation of the mine.
labour_market_demand: The demand for underground mine support workers is Very Good, particularly in provinces with established hard-rock and potash mining industries. In 2026, Ontario (Sudbury/Timmins), Saskatchewan, and Manitoba are facing a critical shortage of support staff. As mines go deeper and become more technologically complex, the need for dedicated workers to manage ventilation and safety infrastructure has become a top priority for Canadian mining firms.
Safety-First Culture: Work in one of the world's most regulated mining environments with a focus on advanced safety protocols.
Technological Exposure: Gain experience with remote-controlled equipment and modern underground environmental monitoring systems.
Rapid Career Growth: Many workers in this NOC quickly transition into high-paying roles as Underground Miners (NOC 83100) after gaining on-the-job experience.
Stable PR Pathways: A recognized "Essential Service" occupation for regional immigration pilots and provincial "Semi-Skilled" streams.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure targeting 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically in essential resource sectors.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for those with job offers from designated mining employers in the Atlantic region.
Saskatchewan INP (Semi-Skilled Agriculture/Natural Resources): Targeted at workers with 6 months of experience in the province.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Accessible for workers in specific mining support roles with an Ontario job offer.
